Name: curvature_aware_dynamic_routing
Title: Dynamic Curvature-Aware Routing for Training-Free Optimization in Machine Learning
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that integrating curvature-aware metrics with a training-free routing framework can dynamically optimize inference paths, enhancing model robustness and efficiency under various operational conditions.
Related Work: Current methods primarily focus on static optimization strategies or heuristic-based dynamic routing. Our approach distinguishes itself by leveraging curvature-aware diagnostics to inform real-time routing decisions, in contrast to existing frameworks that rely on pre-defined heuristics or expensive calibration processes. Specifically, we build on the principles established in 'Spectral Latent Steering' and 'Curvature-Aware Derivative-Free Optimization' but extend them to create a training-free dynamic routing mechanism.
Abstract: In this proposal, we introduce a novel framework for dynamic curvature-aware routing that utilizes geometric diagnostics to optimize inference in machine learning models without the need for training. By analyzing the curvature of the loss landscape and the spectral properties of activations, we can identify optimal routing paths through the model architecture in real-time. This training-free mechanism not only enhances the robustness of models under varying input conditions but also ensures computational efficiency by dynamically adjusting precision and routing based on the local geometry of the input data. We will validate our approach through a series of experiments across various benchmark datasets, demonstrating improvements in accuracy and latency compared to existing static routing methods. Our findings will contribute to the understanding of how curvature-aware metrics can facilitate more adaptive and efficient machine learning systems.
Experiments: 1. Implement a baseline model with static routing using existing heuristics and compare it with the proposed dynamic routing framework.
2. Evaluate the curvature metrics (e.g., Hessian eigenvalues, gradient covariance) in real-time to inform routing decisions.
3. Measure model performance using metrics such as accuracy, latency, and throughput across different datasets and operational conditions.
4. Conduct ablation studies to assess the impact of curvature-aware diagnostics on routing effectiveness.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the complexity of dynamic routing decisions leading to increased overhead if not managed properly. Additionally, the reliance on curvature metrics may not generalize across all model architectures or datasets, requiring careful evaluation and potential adjustments.
